---
title: "Technical how-to guides"
subtitle: "BNR info-hub Technical Manual"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Technical how-to guides

## Purpose

This section contains practical guides for maintaining the BNR `info-hub` infrastructure.

The guides are written for skilled Stata users who may be new to Quarto, Git, GitHub Pages, Python virtual environments, and static website publication.

The normal static briefing pathway is:

```text
run Stata -> inspect output bundle -> copy approved files into the site -> render Quarto -> check HTML/PDF -> update catalogue -> commit and publish
```

These guides focus on the web and publication infrastructure. Registry operations, REDCap procedures, review, sign-off, and disclosure decisions belong in the BNR Operations Manual.

## Where to start

| Task | Guide |
|---|---|
| Set up the repo on a new laptop | [Set up local development](set-up-local-development.qmd) |
| Start routine work | [Start an editing session](start-an-editing-session.qmd) |
| Run a Stata static briefing | [Run a static briefing](run-static-briefing.qmd) |
| Copy approved Stata outputs into the site | [Copy an output bundle](copy-output-bundle.qmd) |
| Render or preview the site | [Render or preview the site](render-site.qmd) |
| Render a PDF briefing | [Render a PDF briefing](render-pdf-briefing.qmd) |
| Update the release catalogue | [Update the release catalogue](update-release-catalogue.qmd) |
| Add another briefing | [Add a new static briefing](add-new-static-briefing.qmd) |
| Create a ZIP package | [Create a public ZIP bundle](create-public-zip-bundle.qmd) |
| Publish the site | [Publish the site](publish-site.qmd) |
| Check files before commit | [Check Git status before commit](check-git-status-before-commit.qmd) |

## Important boundaries

| Folder | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `info-hub/` | Git repository: scripts, Quarto site, documentation, approved public outputs |
| `info-hub-private/` | Local-only private area: confidential data, private prepared datasets, private logs |
| `outputs/public/` | Canonical Stata-generated public output bundles |
| `site/downloads/files/` | Site-accessible copy of approved Stata-generated outputs |
| `site/_site/` | Quarto-rendered site output; generated and ignored by Git |

## Working rule

```text
Stata computes.
Quarto publishes.
Git records only what is safe and intended.
```
